user.title: Set up your Fire TV
user.description:
Enter your Fire TV connection details below.

**Finding your IP address:**
Settings → My Fire TV → About → Network → IP Address

**Enabling ADB Debugging:**
Settings → My Fire TV → Developer Options → ADB Debugging → ON

Both devices must be on the same Wi-Fi network.

data_description.port: Default is 5555, only change if you modified it
data_description.name: Friendly name shown on your dashboard

confirm_tv.title: Approve connection on your TV
confirm_tv.description:
Almost there! When you click **Submit** below, Home Assistant will try to connect to your Fire TV.

**Important:** A USB debugging prompt will appear on your TV screen. You must approve it within 30 seconds using your Fire TV remote.

Make sure your TV is on and you have the remote ready, then click Submit.

error.cannot_connect: Connection failed. Make sure you approved the prompt on your TV screen within 30 seconds. Also verify the IP address is correct and ADB Debugging is enabled. Click Submit to try again.

abort.already_configured: This Fire TV is already configured.

init.title: Fire TV Enhanced Settings
init.description: Configure polling intervals and customize how app names appear on your dashboard. Changes are saved automatically without needing to re-approve on your TV.

data_description.scan_interval: How often to check which app is running (lower = faster, more CPU usage)
data_description.screenshot_interval: How often to capture a new screenshot of the Fire TV screen
data_description.custom_apps_text:
One entry per line using the format: package.name = Display Name
Check the App Package sensor to find package names.

Examples:
com.apple.atv = Apple TV
tv.twitch.android.viewer = Twitch
